briefdescription.para: These are the data structures for each of the boutons.
Flag: Is it on spines or along a dendritic tree?
spacing of presyn boutons along the NeuroMesh (dendrite).
Requires that all objects in v are the head compartments of spines.
Volume of mesh Entry including abutting diff-coupled voxels.

detaileddescription.para: Virtual function to return volume of mesh Entry.

briefdescription.para: Already handled by voxelMidpoint.

detaileddescription.para: Return volumes for each bouton. This is already handled by the inherited function getVoxelVolume, but that is readonly.
Returns xyz triplets for each bouton, for a len of 3*numBoutons Already handled by getVoxelMidpoint Returns index of each bouton on its target compartment For spines all entries will be zero.

briefdescription.para: Spacing between boutons if compartments are dendrite segments.
Virtual function to return coords of mesh Entry.

detaileddescription.para: Virtual function to return coords of mesh Entry. Assume that it is a hemisphere with the flat face toward postsyn. midpoint, direction, diameter.

briefdescription.para: Virtual function to return diffusion X-section area.

detaileddescription.para: Virtual function to return diffusion X-section area for each neighbor There is no diffusion so it is zero.

briefdescription.para: Virtual function to return scale factor for diffusion. 1 here.

detaileddescription.para: Virtual function to return scale factor for diffusion. There is no diffusion so it is zero.

briefdescription.para: Complementary func returning vec of Ids, to match other Meshes.
Virtual function to return dimensions of specified entry.
Virtual function to return volume of mesh Entry.
Virtual function to return MeshType of specified entry.
Report the number of boutons defined on mesh.
Inherited virtual, do nothing for now.

detaileddescription.para: This means the sibling voxel immediately adjacent to it, not the voxel surrounding it. Should we wish to do diffusion we would need to use the parent voxel of the surround voxel. Otherewise use EMTPY_VOXEL == -1U.
Return list of postynaptic compartments, one per bouton. Note that there may be repeats.

briefdescription.para: Already handled by oneVoxelVolume.
Returns the compartments to which each bouton projects.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int index, double &x, double &y, double &z) const
memberdef.name: indexToSpace

detaileddescription.para: Converts specified index to xyz coords of middle of voxel Values out of range return original xyz

memberdef.argsstring: (const Eref &e, double volume, unsigned int numEntries)
memberdef.name: innerBuildDefaultMesh

briefdescription.para: Virtual func to make a mesh with specified Volume and numEntries.
Virtual function to return # of spatial dimensions of mesh.

detaileddescription.para: Inherited virtual func. Returns number of MeshEntry in array

memberdef.argsstring: (const Eref &e, unsigned int numNodes, unsigned int numThreads)
memberdef.name: innerHandleNodeInfo

memberdef.argsstring: (const Eref &e, const SrcFinfo2< unsigned int, vector< double > > *meshStatsFinfo)
memberdef.name: innerHandleRequestMeshStats

param.type: const SrcFinfo2< unsigned int, vector< double > > *

briefdescription.para: More inherited virtual funcs: request comes in for mesh stats.
Inherited virtual func.
Reports if this set of boutons is connected to spines vs dend.

detaileddescription.para: Returns the meshIndices (NOT spatial indices) of all adjacent mesh entry pairs on ether side of the (self, other) junction. meshIndices are the indices that look up entries in the vector of pools. spatialIndices are (iz * ny + iy) * nx + ix, that is, a linear conversion of cartesian spatial indices. So, for two touching cubes, the return vector is the paired meshIndices on either side of the plane of contact. If one mesh has a finer mesh than the other, or if there are more than one contact points from self to other (for example, at a corner), then we just have multiple pairs using the same meshIndex of the repeated voxel.

memberdef.argsstring: (double x, double y, double z, unsigned int &index) const
memberdef.name: nearest

detaileddescription.para: Returns distance and index of nearest mesh entry. Computes each mesh entry position as its geometric centre. If the current location is not inside a valid mesh entry, distance returned is negative.
setRadiusStats is meant to be called before the presynMesh is populated with the chemical system, but after the boutons are created.

briefdescription.para: Inherited virtual. Returns entire volume of compartment.
Virtual func so that derived classes can return voxel midpoint.
Virtual func so that derived classes can pass voxel volume back.
Inherited virtual. Resizes len and dia of each voxel.
We assume the bouton to be a hemisphere.

detaileddescription.para: The PresynMesh is a set of synaptic boutons, all having the same reactions. No diffusion between them so it is easy. Two ways to set up: by matching one-to-one to a list of spines, or by matching many-to-one to dendritic segments.
